title: Mirrored & Pin-Board Changing Screen
summary: A three-panel folding room divider — each panel a double 1x2 frame with a mirror set into one face and a fabric-wrapped pin-board on the other, hinged to fold flat for storage.
description:
Each of the three panels is built from two frames — an inner frame sized to hold a mirror or the pin-board insert, and an outer frame that wraps around it for a finished edge on both faces. Build all three panel pairs identically before assembling anything, since consistent panel sizes are what let the screen fold flat and stand square.

One face of each panel holds a purchased vertical door mirror, set into the inner frame opening. **The other face is a genuine pin-board**, built from cardboard backing wrapped in batting and fabric, stapled taut around the edges before it's set into its own frame opening — this upholstered layer is what gives the board enough give to hold pins.

Hinge the three panels together with 2" hinges so the whole screen folds for storage or moves easily between rooms — this is a genuinely large, flat piece once assembled, so plan hinge placement so the screen folds in a consistent zigzag rather than randomly.

steps.title: Build the inner and outer frames
steps.body: Assemble the three inner frames and three outer frames, sized identically.

steps.title: Build the pin-boards
steps.body: Wrap the cardboard backing with batting and fabric, stapling taut around the edges, then set each into an inner frame opening.

steps.title: Set the mirrors
steps.body: Set a mirror into the opposite face of each panel's inner frame, securing with construction adhesive.

steps.title: Attach the outer frames
steps.body: Nail the outer frame around each inner frame for a finished edge on both faces.

steps.title: Hinge the panels
steps.body: Hinge the three panels together so the screen folds in a consistent zigzag.

unresolvedImages.alt: Mirrored and pin board folding changing screen
